note: H6 brittleness pilot. Two anchors with strong known attractors -- 'pick a word' (serendipity 43%, an open meme) and 'name a tree' (oak 94%, the strongest attractor in the census) -- each run down a perturbation ladder: control, noun typo, verb typo, verb swap, article (a/any), lowercase, politeness, no one-word clamp, terse. If a typo or wording swap shifts the modal, the default is a shallow surface-triggered lookup (supports H1/H2); if serendipity/oak survive, they are deep attractors. English pilot; extend to hi/ur if it bites. Analyze by grouping on the anchor and measuring modal-shift vs control.
